calendar.matters:
Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order
Off Calendar Nov-02-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF ALIGN TECHNOLOGY, INC.'s Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's unopposed "motion for assignment of rights, restraining order, and turnover order" is granted.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-14-2022 Off Calendar

Notice Of Default Prove-Up Hearing
Off Calendar Sep-08-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, being assigned by Department 206 for Prove-Up Hearing as to Defendant Dimensional Dental Management, LLC, a limited liability company.

Dept. 514; Judge Murlene J. Randle; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 11:35:30 a.m., pausing at 11:50:15 a.m., reconvening at 11:50:53 a.m. and concluding at 11:51:45 a.m.

David J. Cook, Esq. appeared on behalf of Plaintiff Align Technology, Inc., a Delaware Corporation.
No appearance by Defendant Dimensional Dental Management, LLC, a limited liability company.

KENT ALLEYNE is sworn to testify on behalf of Plaintiff under direct examination.

Plaintiff Align Technology, Inc., a Delaware Corporation submits the following exhibits for the Court's review:
Exhibit A: List of unpaid invoices totaling $90,559.49

Plaintiff's Exhibit A is ADMITTED INTO EVIDENCE based on the testimony of witness Kent Alleyne.
The matter is submitted.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff Align Technology, Inc., a Delaware Corporation, and against Defendant Dimensional Dental Management, LLC, a limited liability company.
Plaintiff Align Technology, Inc., a Delaware Corporation is awarded judgment against Defendant Dimensional Dental Management, LLC, a limited liability company as follows:
Damages/Principal: $90,559.49
Pre-Judgment Interest: $16,999.55
Costs: $450.00
Total Judgment: $108,009.04

Proposed Judgment was submitted and signed by the Court.
Plaintiff STIPULATES to the return of the exhibits.
